activities_projects.title: Critical Interpretation of the Poem
activities_projects.objective: Develop deeper interpretation of the poem’s message
steps: Read the poem carefully and identify the central message
Analyse how the poet builds the message through events
Explain how the boy’s action represents empathy and humanity
Write a short analytical paragraph explaining the poem’s moral significance
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop analytical thinking and interpret literary messages
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of interpretation and clarity of argument (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Symbolism in the Poem
activities_projects.objective: Understand symbolic elements used by the poet
steps: Identify elements such as winter, snow, and the crowded street
Discuss what these elements symbolise in the poem
Explain how these symbols represent society’s attitude toward the elderly
Present your interpretation in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognise symbolism and interpret deeper meanings
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of interpretation and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Character Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Compare the actions and attitudes of different characters
steps: Describe the behaviour of the group of boys in the poem
Analyse the behaviour of the young boy who helped the woman
Compare these behaviours and explain their moral implications
Write a paragraph discussing how individual actions can influence society
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop comparative analysis and ethical reasoning
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical comparison and supporting arguments (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Exploring Poetic Devices in Depth
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the effect of poetic devices used in the poem
steps: Identify poetic devices such as simile, imagery, and repetition
Explain how each device enhances the emotional tone of the poem
Analyse how these devices influence the reader’s understanding
Write a brief explanation of their literary significance
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how literary techniques shape meaning
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and depth of literary analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Empathy and Social Responsibility Debate
activities_projects.objective: Encourage critical thinking about social responsibility
steps: Form two groups in the class
Debate whether society today shows enough empathy toward the elderly
Use examples from the poem and real-life situations
Conclude with suggestions for improving empathy in society
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical thinking and debating skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of arguments and participation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Imagery and Atmosphere Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand how imagery builds the atmosphere of the poem
steps: Identify phrases describing the winter setting
Explain how the imagery creates a feeling of loneliness and difficulty
Analyse how the environment influences the old woman’s situation
Write a short explanation connecting imagery with the poem’s theme
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyse how setting influences emotional impact

activities_projects.title: Perspective Writing
activities_projects.objective: Develop creative thinking through perspective writing
steps: Imagine you are the old woman in the poem
Write a short reflection describing how you felt before and after the boy helped you
Explain how the boy’s action changed your day
Share your reflection with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop empathy and creative writing skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, emotional depth, and clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Modern Society Reflection
activities_projects.objective: Relate the poem’s message to present-day society
steps: Discuss whether people today help strangers as shown in the poem
Identify challenges that prevent people from helping others
Suggest ways students can encourage kindness in society
Present your ideas in a short class discussion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect literature with modern social issues
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical thinking and originality of ideas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Ethical Decision-Making Activity
steps: Imagine you were one of the boys who ignored the woman
Reflect on why the group did not help her
Describe what you would do differently in that situation
Write a short reflection explaining your decision
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students evaluate ethical choices and moral responsibility
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of reasoning and reflection (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Creative Poetry Extension
activities_projects.objective: Encourage creative expression inspired by the poem
steps: Write a short poem inspired by the theme of kindness
Use at least one poetic device such as repetition or imagery
Share your poem with the class
Explain how your poem reflects the message of helping others
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop creative writing and appreciation of poetic form
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, use of poetic devices, and thematic clarity (10 marks)
